pattern: Reversing the digits is easy; the real task is detecting when the result escapes the signed 32-bit range and returning zero instead.
hints: Peel digits off the end one at a time and rebuild the number; the sign can be handled by working with the magnitude.
After reversing you must reject any value outside the signed 32-bit window - return 0 rather than the overflowing number.
Reverse the absolute value's digits, reapply the sign, and if the result is below -2^31 or above 2^31 - 1, return 0.

edge_inputs.description: largest 32-bit value reversed overflows to zero
reversal that lands exactly in range

stress.description: random 32-bit integers, including many that overflow when reversed
stress.note: size is irrelevant; the value is a single scalar in 32-bit range
